# servicenow_notification (Resource)

`servicenow_notification` manages an email notification (sysevent_email_action) within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages an email notification (sysevent_email_action) in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_notification" "example" {
  name        = "Example Incident Notification"
  table       = "incident"
  event_name  = "incident.inserted"
  active      = true
  description = "Sends an email when a new incident is inserted."
  subject     = "New incident ${number} has been created"
  message_html = <<-EOT
    <p>A new incident has been created.</p>
    <p>Number: $${number}</p>
    <p>Short description: $${short_description}</p>
  EOT
  weight = 100
  type   = "EMAIL"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the email notification.
- `table` (String) Table that the notification fires on.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether or not this notification is enabled.
- `category` (String) sys_id of the notification category record. ServiceNow defaults this from the glide.notification.default_category system property when omitted.
- `condition` (String) Encoded query condition that must be met for the notification to fire.
- `description` (String) Description of the notification and its purpose.
- `event_name` (String) Name of the event that triggers this notification.
- `from` (String) Email address that the notification will be sent from.
- `include_attachments` (Boolean) If true, include attachments from the triggering record on the notification email.
- `mandatory` (Boolean) If true, recipients cannot unsubscribe from this notification.
- `message_html` (String) HTML body of the email notification.
- `message_plain` (String) Plain text body of the email notification.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `recipient_groups` (String) Comma-separated list of group sys_ids that should receive the notification.
- `recipient_roles` (String) Comma-separated list of recipient roles or recipient fields.
- `recipient_users` (String) Comma-separated list of user sys_ids that should receive the notification.
- `reply_to` (String) Reply-to email address for the notification.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `send_self` (Boolean) If true, send the notification to the user that triggered the event.
- `subject` (String) Subject line for the email notification.
- `type` (String) Notification type (e.g. EMAIL).
- `weight` (Number) Numeric weight that controls notification precedence.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_notification.example <sys_id>
```
